chapter: Chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

questions.question: Which institutions provided traditional education in India before British rule?
questions.explanation: Traditional education in India was provided through pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas.

questions.question: Which language did the Anglicists support as the medium of instruction?
questions.explanation: Anglicists believed English should be the medium of instruction.

questions.question: Who supported the teaching of Indian languages and classical texts?
questions.explanation: Orientalists supported Indian languages and classical learning.

questions.question: Who wrote the Minute on Indian Education in 1835?
questions.explanation: Macaulay wrote the famous Minute on Indian Education in 1835.

questions.question: What did Macaulay strongly support?
questions.explanation: Macaulay believed Western knowledge taught through English was superior.

questions.question: Which document is known as the Magna Carta of English education in India?
questions.explanation: Wood’s Despatch of 1854 laid the foundation for modern education in India.

questions.question: Which universities were established in 1857?
questions.explanation: The first modern universities were established in Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras.

questions.question: Which Indian reformer supported modern education and Western knowledge?
questions.explanation: Raja Rammohan Roy supported modern education and scientific learning.

questions.question: What was one major aim of British education policies?
questions.explanation: British policies aimed to spread Western knowledge in India.

questions.question: Which group supported English education in India?
questions.explanation: Anglicists believed English education should be promoted.

questions.question: What type of class emerged due to English education?
questions.explanation: English education led to the rise of an educated middle class.

questions.question: What was one result of Western education in India?
questions.explanation: Western education helped spread ideas of liberty and nationalism.

questions.question: Which subjects were emphasized in Western education?
questions.explanation: Western education promoted subjects such as science and literature.

questions.question: What was one effect of British education policy on traditional schools?
questions.explanation: Traditional institutions declined as English education expanded.

questions.question: What was the main language of higher education introduced by the British?
questions.explanation: English became the primary language of higher education.

questions.question: What role did education play in social reform?

options.B: It promoted awareness and reform ideas

questions.explanation: Education helped reformers challenge social problems.

questions.question: Which concept spread through Western education?
questions.explanation: Western education introduced democratic ideas.

questions.question: Which system introduced universities in India?
questions.explanation: Colonial education introduced modern universities.

questions.question: Which document recommended teacher training institutions?
questions.explanation: Wood’s Despatch recommended teacher training institutions.

questions.question: Why did the British introduce English education?
questions.explanation: The British aimed to create educated Indians who could assist in administration.

questions.question: What kind of knowledge did Orientalists support?
questions.explanation: Orientalists supported classical Indian learning.

questions.question: Which movement benefited from Western education?
questions.explanation: Educated Indians played a major role in the freedom movement.

questions.question: Which city was one of the first university centers in India?
questions.explanation: The University of Calcutta was established in 1857.

questions.question: Which idea spread through modern education in India?
questions.explanation: Modern education spread ideas of equality and rights.

questions.question: Which system influenced the modern Indian education system?
questions.explanation: Colonial education introduced universities, exams, and modern subjects.
